observations.recommendation: Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to observe year-end cutoff procedures by furnishing the Municipal Accounting Office with the Schedule of Unreleased Checks. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the unreleased checks totaling ₱2,693,926.18,
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to inform and require the concerned payees to submit the stale checks issued totaling ₱40,212.61 for possible replacement. Accordingly, an adjusting entry shall be prepared to revert the same to the Payable accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly reconcile the cash balance as stated in the book with the balance in bank statements, determine valid reconciling items and prepare the adjusting or correcting entries, as warranted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Require the Inventory Committee to analyze the nature of the items included in the RPCPPE and classify them according to the appropriate and specific PPE account titles prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts;

Require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory count report and the accounting records. Investigate and evaluate the differences, adjust the records as appropriate and submit a revised RPCPPE based on the reconciliations made;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Direct the Agency’s disposal committee to initiate the disposal procedures for the unserviceable property identified which shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle the disallowance as well as with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: On-going collection efforts by the Management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS;
Perform cash cut-off procedures to ensure the completeness of transactions to be included in the FS;
Conduct a periodic review of outstanding checks in the BRS to detect stale checks and act on them promptly; and
Analyze book reconciling items identified through periodic bank reconciliation and prepare the corresponding journal entries to adjust the book balances. For unidentified bank credits or credit memos, promptly coordinate with the bank to obtain necessary source documents or transaction details in order to facilitate proper classification and timely adjustment in the books.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Inform and require the concerned payees to return the stale checks issued totaling ₱53,491.05 for possible replacement. The stale checks shall be cancelled and reported, with copies attached to the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) prepared during the period of cancellation.  The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI. Henceforth, the office shall send proper written notifications to payees for checks nearing staleness as a preventive control measure; and
Ensure that Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) are prepared for all undeposited cash collections as of December 31 and furnish these reports to the Accounting Office for proper recognition.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineering Office, in coordination with the General Services Officer Designate to segregate construction materials by category and ensure each group is clearly labeled for easy identification. Designate specific areas or use shelves and racking systems where practicable within the warehouse for different material types (e.g., cement, wood, pipes) and ensure that all large and heavy materials are securely stacked to prevent tipping or collapse.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments
correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS;
Direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory count report and the accounting records. Investigate and evaluate the differences, adjust the records as appropriate and submit a revised RPCPPE based on the reconciliations made; and
Instruct the Municipal General Services Office, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, to prepare Inventory and IIRUP and furnish a copy to the Accounting Office. Henceforth, the Accounting Office shall conduct impairment testing for unserviceable property not yet disposed as of year-end based on submitted IIRUP and recognize impairment losses, if any.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to identify the Accountable Officer and file the required “Request for Relief from Accountability” detailing the facts and circumstances of the loss and with compliance to the documentary requirements set forth in COA Memorandum No. 1992-751; and
After the determination of the accountable person and the filing of the request for relief from accountability, require the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry by debiting “Due from Officers and Employees” and crediting “Breeding Stocks” for ₱10,600.00.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly review the nature of each transaction and ensure the proper recording of accounts to reflect the correct presentation in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the NFS all the contingent liability arising from the undelivered POs at year-end to ensure the fair presentation of the FS.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct a thorough review on the outstanding Guaranty Deposit Payable account to determine the nature and validity of the balances, and to establish and implement regular monitoring and reconciliation procedures to promptly identify and address long-outstanding balances; and
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office to formally notify and coordinate with suppliers and contractors with valid claims, requesting them to submit the necessary documents for the prompt processing and release of their warranty and retention deposits.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the nature of the abnormal balance noted on the “Government Equity” account in the Trust Fund, prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, ensure that the FS are free from errors due to inappropriate posting.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare working paper adjustment entries for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction with due consideration on the expense recognition principles set by the applicable IPSAS and COA rules and regulations and use the correct account titles.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Human Resource Management Officer to:
Coordinate with the PHIC to validate and reconcile the outstanding premium contributions totaling ₱94,459.38, including the details of the applicable employees, corresponding payroll periods, and supporting documents; and
If confirmed to be unpaid, take prompt and appropriate action to settle the outstanding premium contributions, including any applicable interest and penalties, in strict compliance with the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s.2014 and other relevant regulations and issuances. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare adjusting entries based on the results of the reconciliation with the PHIC.

We commended the Management's efforts in the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF. Management shall continue to monitor the implementation of PPAs to sustain a high utilization rate, thereby strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person:
Ensure that all PPAs are well-planned by providing a rigid review and research of specific guidelines set by law on GAD-related issues and concerns;
Regularly monitor and review the implementation of GAD PPAs and make adjustments as necessary in case of unexpected circumstances, following the guidelines prescribed under JMC No. 2013-01; and
Conduct close coordination with other offices and stakeholders to ensure that programs are properly undertaken.
Henceforth, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of GAD program, projects and activities to fully utilize the GAD Plans and Budget.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly observe the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to present prior period errors and adjustments due to changes in accounting policy by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown in the corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance to IPSAS 3

Include as a priority project the establishment of a room/building which shall serve as the storage facility for procured property and inventories prior to the release to end-user units. Relative to this, Management shall endeavor to maintain a centralized inventory management system;
observations.managementAction: Organized a room to serve as a storage facility, centralizing inventory in one location.

observations.recommendation: Pending the establishment of a storage room/warehouse, the Municipal Mayor shall designate employees, who shall be properly bonded, to manage the issuance, recording and reporting of inventories in their respective offices;
observations.managementAction: A bonded employee has already been designated to manage the issuance, recording, and reporting of inventories.

observations.recommendation: Require the proper utilization of the stock cards at the office-level in the monitoring of supplies and materials

Require the Agency’s Inventory Committee together with the representatives from the end-user units and the General Services Officer to conduct an inventory count on all office supplies that are yet to be consumed as of a cut-off date to be determined by the Committee and prepare a Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for recording and control purposes. Henceforth, conduct the required physical count and submit to the Auditor the report thereon not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;

Based on the RPCI prepared, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to analyze the nature of every transaction and withhold the correct amount of tax on income payments to suppliers pursuant to the provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC), as amended and other BIR issuances and remit the same to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Withheld the proper withholding tax from suppliers

observations.recommendation: Submit explanation/ justification with verifiable basis why disbursements on procurements which were noted as non-compliant with existing procurement law, as mentioned above, should not be suspended/disallowed in post-audit;
observations.managementAction: The management explained during the exit conference the reasons for non-compliance with the existing procurement law.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements for the procurement of goods and services are included and updated in the end-user PPMP and Agency APP by requiring a certification to that effect from the Head of the BAC Secretariat, who shall, based on the submissions of the end-user units, validate such fact;
observations.managementAction: Directed the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements for goods and services procurement are included and updated in the end-user PPMP and Agency APP, with verification required from the end-user units.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, require the BAC to submit the proof of validation on the technical, legal and financial capability of the suppliers together with Mayor’s Permit and proof of PhilGEPs Registration on all purchases made through Alternative Mode of Procurement to support eligibility of suppliers;

Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to specify in the Request for Quotation (RFQ) under the General Instructions portion the evaluation method for submitted bids and indicating whether the contract will be awarded on a per item, whole lot, or by several lots. Subsequently, ensure that the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) reflected in the RFQ accurately corresponds to the chosen bid evaluation method, providing comprehensive information to interested bidder-suppliers about the bid ceiling;

Direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that posting requirements of RFQ, Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract and Notice to Proceed, if necessary, on all procurement through shopping with ABC higher than P50,000.00 are complied with and proof thereof are attached to the disbursement voucher to establish correctness of information and reliability of bidding process; and

Ensure that all regulatory permits, registrations and licenses are available and up-to-date prior to engaging the services of individuals and businesses. If unavailable, encourage the prospective suppliers to secure permits and comply other registration requirements such as Mayor’s Permit, BIR and PhilGEPS registrations etc., to ensure eligibility in future procurement endeavors with the LGU-Ivana.
observations.managementAction: Ensuring that all regulatory permits, registrations, and licenses are current before engaging individuals and businesses. Encouraged prospective suppliers to secure permits and fulfill registration requirements (e.g., Mayor’s Permit, BIR, PhilGEPS) to ensure eligibility for future procurement with LGU-Ivana.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that PPAs funded out of the DRRM funds are well-planned and meticulously reviewed by the LDRRMC. Accordingly, intensive procurement planning and coordination with stakeholders and regulating agencies shall be conducted prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP;

Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, in coordination with the implementing offices, to strictly monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) and formulate catch-up plans for slow to non-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation and facilitate the timely completion thereof;

Require the implementing office, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee for purposes of monitoring compliance, to timely identify the component items of the crafted PPAs and religiously submit the corresponding PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation or updating of APP, to prevent LDRRM Funds to became idle, thus, maximum utilization can be achieved.

Utilize the stock card in the inventory management to ensure that welfare goods are available and in optimal condition when required. Similarly, ensure the timely and proper disposal of nearly expiring goods/items through the identified modality by the LGU, to preclude any wastage of government funds; and
Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to present prior period errors and adjustments due to changes in accounting policy by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown in the corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance to IPSAS 3.

Require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to determine timely the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to observe the timely procurement activities to ensure prompt delivery of materials and other items necessary in the implementation of PPAs, thus reducing the risk of delayed implementation. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to conduct early procurement activities pursuant to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act;

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bid and Awards Committee and Budget Office, to consider the inflation rate and history of prices in the program/project costing to preclude series of failures of bidding, thereby expediting the procurement activities and deliveries of materials needed in the implementation of the PPAs; and

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that PPAs funded out of the DRRM funds are well-planned and meticulously reviewed by the LDRRMC. Accordingly, intensive procurement planning and coordination with stakeholders shall be conducted prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP;
Strictly monitor its implementation to facilitate the timely completion thereof;

Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, in coordination with the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC), to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to determine timely the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated to finance new or augment existing Disaster Risk Reduction Management PPAs and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

Require the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review the existing PPAs under the LDRRMF to determine the relevance and responsiveness of the projects to the present circumstances including the reasonableness of project costing and make the necessary revisions, as warranted, subject to the appropriate approvals;

We recommended that Management strictly observe the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Restated in Part II-Observations and Recommendations of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle the disallowance as well as with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: On-going collection efforts by the Management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to observe year-end cutoff procedures by furnishing the Municipal Accounting Office with the Schedule of Unreleased Checks. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the unreleased checks totaling ₱2,693,926.18, as follows:
Require the Municipal Accountant to inform and require the concerned payees to submit the stale checks issued totaling ₱40,212.61 for possible replacement. Accordingly, an adjusting entry shall be prepared to revert the same to the Payable accounts, as follows:
Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly reconcile the cash balance as stated in the book with the balance in bank statements, determine valid reconciling items and prepare the adjusting or correcting entries, as warranted.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management strictly observe the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to present prior period errors and adjustments due to changes in accounting policy by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown in the corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance to IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to analyze the nature of every transaction and withhold the correct amount of tax on income payments to suppliers pursuant to the provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC), as amended and other BIR issuances and remit the same to the BIR.

Require the Inventory Committee to analyze the nature of the items included in the RPCPPE and classify them according to the appropriate and specific PPE account titles prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts;
Require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to identify the PPE items that failed to meet the ₱15,000.00 capitalization and adjust the appropriate records, as warranted;
Require the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to ensure that the identified and recorded PPE items of the same nature are not taken as one instead segregated on a per item basis; and

Require the Agency’s Inventory Committee, Appraisal Committee in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to ascertain the fair value less cost to sell of the identified biological assets and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Accounting Office;

Direct the Municipal Accountant, based on the report to be prepared by the concerned personnel, to prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the “Breeding Stocks” accounts. Henceforth, biological assets, at each reporting date, shall be measured at fair value less cost to sell as required in IPSAS 27; and

Henceforth, advise the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist to regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the biological assets of the Agency to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the biological assets.

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end.

Require the Municipal Accounting to ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CO shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefor are completed as prescribed under Item 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to file an application for bonding with the Bureau of the Treasury-Batanes Provincial Office, strictly monitor the expiration of bond coverage and apply for renewal thereof prior to its expiration as required in Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office (MAccO) and the Property Office to reconcile results of the physical inventory taking as reflected in the RPCPPE with their respective records. Require the accounting office to prepare necessary adjusting entries for any discrepancies supported by an evidence to present an accurate amount as reflected in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented.
observations.reason: Restated in paragraph 4, Part II-Observations and Recommendations of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate journal entries in recording the net adjustment totaling P3,374,446.70 , duly supported by verifiable evidence such as schedules and documents. Relative to this, all valid book reconciling items shall be recorded at year-end to enhance the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to investigate the nature of the abnormal balances noted on the liability accounts, prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, ensure that the financial statements are free from errors due to inappropriate posting.

We recommended that Management:
provide an explanation/justification with verifiable basis why the payment of Calamity/Pandemic Relief Assistance for CYs 2020 and 2021 which was noted as non-compliant with existing laws and COA rules and regulations, as mentioned above, should not be disallowed in post-audit. Otherwise, require the immediate refund of the amount received by all payees; and
discontinue granting benefits to its employees without sufficient and valid legal basis.

We recommended that Management:
submit the following documents as additional basis in audit:
Training Matrix for the Municipal and Barangay task Force Seminar
Pictures taken during the activities
Proof that the Notice of meeting was received by the intended participants
LDRRMF Work and Financial Plan for 2021
BIR Certificate of Registration of the supplier;
henceforth, prior to the preparation of the Purchase Request, an Activity Request duly approved by the Municipal Mayor shall be prepared showing the linkage of the activities to the specific programs/projects of the office as shown in the Annual Investment Program (AIP), expected output/s to be produced from the activities, and financial requirements and other resources and other information that may support the validity of the charging for meals and snacks;
submit explanation/justification with verifiable basis to support the validity and propriety of the transactions and why disbursements that show apparent inconsistencies and deficiencies should not be suspended/disallowed in audit; and
strictly require the submission of complete documentary requirements in the disbursements of government funds

We recommended that Management:
submit explanation/justification with verifiable basis why inclusion of laptop on the procurement of materials charged against infrastructure project should not be suspended/disallowed in audit; and
direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly implement existing regulations on the preparation of Program of Work.

We recommended that Management:
submit an explanation/justification with verifiable basis why the claims of mobile load and internet expenses which was deemed excessive should not be suspended/disallowed in audit; and
establish an internal policy on the grant of mobile load and internet expenses which shall not exceed what is usual and proper and shall not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by telecommunication services. The policy shall stipulate the officials and employees who are entitled thereon and the documentary requirements to support the disbursement.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Engineer to submit the documents to support variation order as per Item 1.5.a of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and provide justifications in case of absence thereof;
provide justification on the noted discrepancies on the amount of original contract between the Statement of the Time Elapsed and Work Accomplished and the Monthly Certificate of Payment; and
advise all officials and employees involved in the implementation of contracts to strictly observe the procedural and documentary requirements prescribed by RA 9184 and its Revised IRR and COA Issuances.

We recommended that Management:
instruct the LDRRM Officer to include in its LDRRMFIP  for the current year, in a separate section, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years of the Special Trust Fund; and
require the LDRRMO to  properly plan and identify projects which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.

We recommended that Management:
direct all individual service providers under Job Order who are deriving income below P250,00.00 to execute an Income Payee’s Sworn Declaration of gross receipts/sales (Annex B2 of RR 11-2018 or Annex A1 of RMC 51-2018) and pay the Annual Registration Fee the proofs of which shall be submitted to the Municipal Human Resource Management Office;
require the authorized representative of the Agency, after submission by the service providers of the duly accomplished declaration, to execute the Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex C of RR 11-2018) and submit together with the list of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
require the Municipal Human Resource Office to strictly observe the annual compliance deadlines as prescribed by RR 11-2018. Accordingly, the requirements shall be submitted by the individual service providers before January 15 or prior to initial income payment, whichever is applicable. The documentary requirements of the Agency as withholding agent shall be submitted to the BIR  office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration to the lone income payor/withholding agent.

We recommended that Management require the identified officials and employees to file an application for bonding with the Bureau of the Treasury-Batanes Provincial Office, strictly monitor the expiration of bond coverage and apply for renewal thereof prior to its expiration as required in Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 with due consideration on the processing time from the application to the approval of the bond.

We recommended that Management would carefully validate the eligibility of the list of targeted beneficiaries/clients to avoid duplication in the benefits granted and to adhere strictly to the aforementioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle the disallowance as well as with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction:
Partially implemented.

The amount of ₱10,000.00 was settled in the 3rd Quarter of 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the priority projects funded from national agencies and monitor the utilization of the funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement (MOA) with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining unutilized amount on programs/projects which have already been completed should be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Some unutilized amount from completed programs/projects were already returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that Management would validate carefully the eligibility of the list of targeted beneficiaries/clients to avoid duplication in the benefits granted and to adhere strictly to the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended  Management to continue to disbursed funds received from national agency in accordance to the specific purpose of the fund downloaded. Any unutilized balance if any after the program, project and activities undertaken must be returned to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle the disallowance as well as with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office (MAccO) and the Property Office to reconcile results of the physical inventory taking as reflected in the RPCPPE with their respective records. Require the accounting office to prepare necessary adjusting entries for any discrepancies supported by an evidence to present an accurate amount as reflected in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management implement the priority projects funded from national agencies and monitor the utilization of the funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement (MOA) with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining unutilized amount on programs/projects which have already been completed should be returned to the source agency.

We recommended to submit the necessary reports and documents mentioned above to aid us in audit and return the unutilized/excess balances, if any, in compliance with Section 4.6 and 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that management implement the priority projects funded from national agencies and monitor the utilization of the funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement (MOA) with the source agency.

We recommended timely implementation of the priority projects funded from Local Government Support Fund (LGSF)-Financial Assistance in order to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time. Likewise, the remaining unutilized amount on programs/projects which have already been completed should be returned to the source agency.

We reiterate our recommendation on PY 2018 AAR that management prepare and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law. It is further recommended that a focal person be designated by the Local Chief Executive who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.

We recommended and management agreed that the municipality submit its GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for the ensuing year to the DILG for review and approval pursuant to above mentioned rules and regulations.
We recommended that the management, particularly GFPS, comply with the full disclosure policy through annual preparation, submission and posting of GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices adhere to Section 7.2 of COA Circular 2009-006 and certain provision of PD 1445 on the prompt submission of reports and documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts and effect the necessary, adjustments to revert to the appropriate fund the balance of prior years’ Accounts Payable which are not supported with valid claims. Ensure payable must be recognized only based on present obligations and supported with complete and valid documents.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to insure all of the provincial properties particularly the motor vehicles with the General Insurance Fund of GSIS, otherwise provide/submit necessary documents to justify the action of not insuring some of the motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: All of the said motor vehicles were insured at GSIS except for the two(2) motorcycles due to the reason that the plate number is not yet available

We recommended management to provide the audit team of a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement. observations.managementAction: The management has submitted implementation schedule for project in accordance to MOA, hence identified project completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that grant of professional fees to the Doctor to the Barrio in the succeeding months shall be withheld with the corresponding tax taking into consideration compliance with the documentary requirement in determining the applicable rate as stipulated in the provisions of Section 2 of RR No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the auditors recommendation to deduct the tax withheld and assured the audit team adopt the provisions of BIR in the succeeding transactions
The management prioritize the implementation of the identified PAPs in the 20% DF, however review of the accomplishment report submitted showed partial implementation on some PAPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit the lacking supporting documents immediately to avoid possible issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance of these transactions. Furthermore, Officials and employees in-charge of the processing and approval of claims  should see to it that all required supporting documents are duly intact and attached to the disbursement vouchers with all the necessary data and information.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to make the necessary adjustments of the erroneous recording of the receipt of fund and to record succeeding similar transactions in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management prepared the adjusting entry in the books of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal LDRRMA include in the LDRRMFIP, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years of the Trust Fund. We also recommend the implementation of the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the recommendation of the audit team

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend Management to:

Deduct from every progress payment portion of the advance payment to be recouped;
Deduct from every progress payment the retention money until fifty percent (50%) of the value of works, as determined by the Municipal Government, are completed. If after 50% completion the work is satisfactorily done and on schedule, no additional retention shall be made; otherwise, the ten percent (10%) retention shall be imposed.
Evaluate and properly assess the actual accomplishment of the project to ascertain the correct amount to be paid to the contractor and avoid overpayment.
observations.managementAction: The management evaluated the said project, hence project completed in the first phase and revert the remaining balance to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the inventory committee should properly classify the specific inventory type in the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and exert all efforts to reconcile their records with the Municipal Accounting Office to determine the actual balances of the property, plant and equipment. Any discrepancies between the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and the accounting records should be investigated and adjusted accordingly and see to it that all properties issued to its employees are covered by an updated Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to pinpoint accountability over the properties.
observations.managementAction: The management conducted physical inventory and to conduct reconciliation of report between the GSO and accounting office. However review of the submitted reports showed partial implementation of the said audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to reconcile the negative balances of RPT/SET Receivables and effect the necessary adjusting entries in the agency books to record the correct amount in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management has taken action in the necessary adjustment of RPT/SET accounts in the agency books in order to reflect the correct amount in the financial statement

observations.recommendation: We recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the details/information on the unexpended balances of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation to disclosed the details/information on the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the notes to the Financial Statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive together with the member of the Municipal Waste Management Board to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management assured the audit team that the establishment of sanitary landfill is on going. They still work out on the necessary requirements needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive together with the member of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU did not implement the audit recommendation due to insufficient funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the information on the unexpended balances of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendation to disclose in the notes to financial statement the details of unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to (a) submit all the required documentary requirements for the accreditation and constitution of the CNA before payment of incentive as provided under Budget Circular No. 2017-3; and (b) report signed by the Local Finance Committee as basis for declaring savings for the year showing the budgeted amount, actual expenditures and balances per accounts; (c) ensure that documents are complete before transactions are processed and recorded to avoid possible issuance of notice of suspension that eventually could mature into disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management has not submitted yet all the required documents recommended by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit legal basis for granting Economic Relief Assistance. Likewise, discontinue the practice of granting allowances which do not have legal basis to avoid possible issuance of disallowances. More so, submit authority from the DBM and/or the Office of the President for concurrence of Resolution as to allowable amount entitled to be given to each Municipal officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management have not yet submitted their legal basis for granting Economic Relief Assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office exert all efforts to reconcile their records to determine the actual balances of the property, plant and equipment. Any discrepancies between the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and the accounting records should be investigated and adjusted accordingly and see to it that all properties issued to its employees are covered by an updated Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to pinpoint accountability over the properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the persons liable/responsible for disallowances to settle their accounts as provided in the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Officials and Employees that are persons liable to the Notice of Disallowance were continuously deducting through salary deductions.

We further recommend to management the imposition of liquidated damages to the contractors for failure on their part to complete the projects within the specified contract time.

We recommended that the management facilitate the immediate implementation of the projects, programs, and activities funded from 20% Development Fund in the years these were programmed to allow timely delivery of basic services and benefits to residents and constituents of the municipality.

We recommended management to submit the lacking supporting documents immediately to avoid possible issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance of these transactions. Furthermore, Officials and employees in-charge of the processing and approval of claims  should see to it that all required supporting documents are duly intact and attached to the disbursement vouchers with all the necessary data and information.

We recommended Management to make the necessary adjustments of the erroneous recording of the receipt of fund and to record succeeding similar transactions in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

We therefore recommend Management to:
Deduct from every progress payment portion of the advance payment to be recouped;
Deduct from every progress payment the retention money until fifty percent (50%) of the value of works, as determined by the Municipal Government, are completed. If after 50% completion the work is satisfactorily done and on schedule, no additional retention shall be made; otherwise, the ten percent (10%) retention shall be imposed.
Evaluate and properly assess the actual accomplishment of the project to ascertain the correct amount to be paid to the contractor and avoid overpayment.

We recommended that the inventory committee should properly classify the specific inventory type in the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and exert all efforts to reconcile their records with the Municipal Accounting Office to determine the actual balances of the property, plant and equipment. Any discrepancies between the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and the accounting records should be investigated and adjusted accordingly and see to it that all properties issued to its employees are covered by an updated Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to pinpoint accountability over the properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to reconcile the negative balances of RPT/SET Receivables and effect the necessary adjusting entries in the agency books to record the correct amount in the financial statements.

We recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the details/information on the unexpended balances of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive together with the member of the Municipal Waste Management Board to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to follow the procedures in the disbursement of funds for the acquisition of equipment. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to pay transactions of this nature through checks for control purposes.
observations.managementAction: The management is now strictly following proper procedure in the payment of supplies and equipment thru check in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer settle the unliquidated balance of the fund as required by Section 48 of the Manual on the New Government ,Accounting System, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management has fully liquidated the Petty Cash Fund as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to register government motor vehicles on time to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the recommendation to register its government vehicles on time to avoid penalties. In cases where penalties were incurred, the person responsible/accountable for the equipment shoulders the penalty.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal officials, employees and the Municipal Accountant comply strictly with the provisions of the above mentioned rules and regulations for proper control of disbursements on government funds and to submit required supporting documents of those officials and employees who claimed more than ten (10) days as well as those claims that were 50% or more  of their total earned leave credits to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Suspension that eventually would lead to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with our recommendation to strictly follow rules and regulation for proper control of disbursements on government funds pertaining to the payment of monetization of earned leave credits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with our recommendation to submit the monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the acting Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in a Special Trust Fund in order to ensure and facilitate the continued use of the fund for disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP within the next five years.
observations.managementAction: Management has transferred the unexpended portion of the current year appropriation of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 dated August 28, 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned department heads to submit to the Auditor their action plans and status of implementation in the 2016 AAR findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: Management have submitted its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation on Jan 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to (a) follow strictly the approved Program of Work in the implementation of its projects; (b) stop the practice of procuring aggregates through labor payrolls; and  (c) adhere strictly with the above cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with our audit recommendation and stopped the practice of procuring aggregates through labor payroll.
Management has not submitted yet all the required documents recommended by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal government submit its GPB for the ensuing year to the DILG office for review and approval pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. 9.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned department heads to submit to the Auditor their action plans and status of implementation in the 2016 AAR findings and recommendations.

12.7 We recommended Management to (a) submit all the required documentary requirements for the accreditation and constitution of the CNA before payment of incentive as provided under Budget Circular No. 2017-3; and (b) report signed by the Local Finance Committee as basis for declaring savings for the year showing the budgeted amount, actual expenditures and balances per accounts; (c) ensure that documents are complete before transactions are processed and recorded to avoid possible issuance of notice of suspension that eventually could mature into disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) management observe the provision of Section 48 of the General Appropriation Act (GAA) of 2015 in the preparation of payroll. Stop deduction which are not allowable by existing regulation so that employees monthly net take home pay will reach the required minimum balance of P3,000.00. Require the Acting Municipal accountant advise the municipal officers and employees on their net take home pay when applying for renewal of loans so that the minimum net take home pay as mandated by law will be observed.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with our audit observation to monitor the minimum monthly take home pay of its personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly follow the provision of item 1.2.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the replenishment of petty cash fund

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the terms and condition of the MOA signed by the Province and the Municipalities by submitting Liquidation Reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU prioritize the appointment of a Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget and Municipal Engineer, with qualifications as provided under the Local Government Code, to improve the financial management, financial reporting system and project management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU Ivana posted the availability of mandatory position as of year-end. The position of Municipal Budget and Municipal Engineer were duly filled up by the qualified applicants.
observations.reason: They already have a full pledge budget officer and Municipal Engineer

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management follow strictly its provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Management re-aligned the said fund to infrastructure projects which will be beneficial to its constituents

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal person in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to formulate an extensive GAD plan consisting of specific projects and programs designed to address gender issues and concerns to maximize results that contribute to economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with our audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) request the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all properties and equipment and to reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to ascertain the accuracy and validity of the reported balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted physical count of property, plant and equipment last January 12, 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the Municipal accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period as required by the above-mentioned rules and regulations for verification.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of the submission of Disbursement Vouchers, Trail Balance & Other required reports disclosed that the Local Government Unit6 were able to submit regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the persons liable/responsible for disallowances to settle their accounts as provided in the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Review and verification of the accounts disclosed that staggered payment of Notice of Disallowance were made thru salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality mark its official vehicles with “For Official Use Only” for control purposes and to prevent the possible abuse or misuse of the same. Likewise, we recommended that government vehicles should be parked within the government premises after office hours.
observations.managementAction: Management already complied with our audit recommendations. Government motor vehicles were properly mark with “For Official Use Only”.
